identity.short_name: Minimal time adversary can block when avoidance starts at step 0

problem_spec.summary: On a finite grid, a walker makes unit taxicab steps with a no-backtrack constraint. An adversary walks as well, and at each step n the pair {b_n,b_{n+1}} is forbidden for both endpoints of the walker's step n (including n=0). Determine the minimal time t for which the adversary can preselect a path b_1,...,b_t that makes any length-t walker path impossible.
givens: Walker dynamics: for all n>=0, d(a_{n+1},a_n)=1 and no-backtrack a_{n+2}\neqa_n.
Adversary dynamics: for all n>=0, d(b_{n+1},b_n)=1.
Avoidance rule: for each step n=0,1,...,t-1, both endpoints satisfy a_n∉B_n and a_{n+1}∉B_n.
ask: Find the minimal t such that some adversary choice of (b_n) makes it impossible for any (a_n) to satisfy all constraints through step t-1; if none exists, output 999.

parameter(s).name: forbidden_block_size
parameter(s).purpose: each B_n is two adjacent points

parameter(s).name: avoidance_start_step
parameter(s).purpose: avoidance applies beginning at step 0 (key mutation from the source family)

invariant(s).name: local_avoidance_constraint
invariant(s).statement: For each step n=0..t-1, the walker's endpoints must lie in G\B_n, i.e., B_n deletes up to two vertices for that step.

invariant(s).name: finite_state_reduction
invariant(s).statement: The walker's feasible state at step n can be represented by the directed edge (a_{n-1},a_n), since the next move is any neighbor of a_n except a_{n-1}, subject to avoidance constraints.

invariant(s).name: manhattan_reachability_bound
invariant(s).statement: After k adversary moves, b_k lies within taxicab distance k of b_0, so every point in B_n lies within distance \leq n+1 of b_0. This yields "safe" cells that cannot appear in early blocked pairs.

invariant(s).name: adversary_threshold_definition
invariant(s).statement: Minimal t is characterized by existence of a b-sequence killing all a-paths by time t and nonexistence for t-1.

essential_assumption(s): The adversary's sequence (b_n) is fixed in advance (adversarial existential quantifier over sequences).
Avoidance is enforced for steps n=0..t-1 (includes the first step).
No-backtrack is strict: immediate reversal a_{n+2}=a_n is forbidden.
uniqueness_certificate(s): An explicit adversary path that blocks all walker paths by time t gives an upper bound on the minimal t. A single explicit walker path that succeeds up to time t-1 for every admissible adversary path gives a lower bound. Matching bounds uniquely determine the minimal t.

technique(s).name: universal_safe_path_for_lower_bound
technique(s).purpose: build a fixed walker path that avoids all possible early blocked pairs using distance-to-b0 arguments

technique(s).name: explicit_adversary_construction_for_failure
technique(s).purpose: provide a concrete adversary path whose induced blocked pairs force a contradiction for any walker path

technique(s).name: local_forcing_case_analysis
technique(s).purpose: prune walker possibilities step-by-step until a forced forbidden endpoint occurs

theorem(s).name: grid_graph_walk_model
theorem(s).role: Manhattan-distance-1 steps are edges in the grid graph

theorem(s).name: memory_1_walk_constraint
theorem(s).role: no-backtrack makes the walker's state a directed edge

theorem(s).name: manhattan_ball_reachability
theorem(s).role: after k unit steps, a point is within distance k of the start

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: view the process as a finite-state reachability game and use distance constraints to find safe trajectories.

critical_trick(s): Use a "safe-zone" distance argument to produce a universal walker path surviving 4 steps (lower bound).
Give a concrete adversary path whose moving blocked pair forces the walker into a unique continuation and then a forbidden endpoint (upper bound).

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: optional brute-force enumeration of adversary paths and DP verification on the finite state space

task(s).name: brute_force_min_t
task(s).reason: confirm minimal t by enumerating all b-paths up to a horizon and checking DP-feasibility of a

task(s).name: extract_witness_paths
task(s).reason: find one blocking b-path at t and one universal-feasible a-path at t-1
